chapter_context.summary: Rebukes the proud Prince of Tyre who claims to be a god but will die a human death, delivers a lament over the King of Tyre, describing his former state in Eden adorned with precious stones until pride was found in him, and prophesies against Sidon.

outline.text: Rebuking the Prince of Tyre: 'Thou art a man, and not God'
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Rebuking the Prince of Tyre: 'Thou art a man, and not God' (28:1-10), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

outline.text: Lament for King of Tyre: Anointed Cherub Cast Out of Eden
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Lament for King of Tyre: Anointed Cherub Cast Out of Eden (28:11-19), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

outline.text: Prophecy of Disease and Desolation Against Sidon
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Prophecy of Disease and Desolation Against Sidon (28:20-24), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

outline.text: Promise of Israel's Gathering and Peaceful Dwelling
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Promise of Israel's Gathering and Peaceful Dwelling (28:25-26), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

key_themes.title: Prophecies Against Pagan Nations
key_themes.description: This chapter forms a core part of Ezekiel's prophecies concerning prophecies against pagan nations, illustrating God's absolute sovereignty in both justice and final restoration.

key_themes.title: The Glory of the Lord
key_themes.description: A central theological theme in Ezekiel, describing the overwhelming majesty of God's presence, its departure due to sin, and its return in future restoration.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the visions, symbolic actions, and warnings of Chapter 28, what does this teach you about God's holiness and His desire for deep, covenant integrity in your own life today?
How does this chapter encourage you to rely on the Spirit's empowerment to stand on your feet and witness faithfully in a challenging culture?

cultural_historical.title: The Exile in Babylon
cultural_historical.text: Ezekiel ministered in exile among the deportees by the River Chebar, providing a crucial priestly voice that helped preserve Israel's faith during the collapse of the monarchy and the destruction of the Temple.
